title: What Did You Do Yesterday?
learningGoal: The learner will compose 3 sentences utilizing the Simple Past tense, successfully appending '-ed' to regular verbs, deploying irregular alternants (went/ate), and rigorously omitting auxiliary 'to be' verbs.
instructions: Think about what you did yesterday. Write 3 short sentences about it. You must use the words 'Went', 'Ate', and one word ending in '-ed' (like Played, Walked, or Watched). REMEMBER THE GOLDEN RULE: Do not use Is, Am, or Are! Just 'I went', 'I ate'!

contentItems.text: CRITICAL RULE: DO NOT WRITE 'I am went'. That means 'मैं गया हूँ (अजीब तरीके से)'. Just write 'I went'.

writingPrompt: Write 3 distinct sentences describing your activities yesterday. One sentence must use 'Went'. One sentence must use 'Ate'. One sentence must use an '-ed' verb.
promptGoal: Test generation of past tense affirmative syntax strictly devoid of auxiliary contamination.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: I went to the store. I ate an apple. I watched television.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: आई वेंट टू द स्टोर। आई एट ऐन ऐपल। आई वाच्ड टेलीविज़न।
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: मैं स्टोर गया। मैंने एक सेब खाया। मैंने टेलीविजन देखा।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect score. The learner used 'went', 'ate', and an '-ed' verb perfectly, and completely omitted 'am' or 'was'.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: I am went to the park. I eated pizza. I played football.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: आई एम वेंट टू द पार्क। आई ईटेड पिज़्ज़ा। आई प्लेड फुटबॉल।
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: मैं पार्क गया। मैंने पिज़्ज़ा खाया। मैंने फुटबॉल खेला।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Critical failure. Generated 'I am went' (auxiliary contamination) and 'I eated' (over-regularization). Needs review.

evaluationCriteria.description: PENALIZE HEAVILY if the user includes 'am', 'is', 'are' before the past tense verb.
Did the user correctly use 'went' and 'ate' rather than 'goed' and 'eated'?
Did the user successfully append '-ed' to a regular verb base for the third sentence?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: I went to the big park. I ate a good sandwich. I walked to my house.
acceptableAlternates: Yesterday I went to school. I ate my lunch. I played with my friends.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Absolute zero tolerance for 'I am [past verb]'. Absolute zero tolerance for 'goed' or 'eated'.

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You successfully talked about yesterday! Most importantly, you didn't accidentally use 'I am'. You are breaking your Hindi grammar habits perfectly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please review what you wrote. Did you write 'I am went'? 'I am' means 'मैं हूँ'. So 'I am went' means 'मैं हूँ गया'. You just say 'I went'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good start! Double check your spelling. Remember, 'Go' and 'Eat' are rule-breakers. You cannot write 'Goed' or 'Eated'. It must be 'Went' and 'Ate'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Write three sentences about yesterday. Use Went, Ate, and one word ending in ED. Do not use 'Am'.

hintText: Don't overthink it! [I] + [Action Word]. 'I went. I played. I ate'. It is that simple!
